text.title: ddd-aggregates Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for ddd-aggregates. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: The Aggregate Root MUST be the only entry point for mutations: external code MUST NOT reach into child Entities or Value Objects and mutate them directly. All state changes happen through named command methods on the root.
rule.rationale: Direct mutation bypasses invariant checks. Intention-revealing methods (place, cancel, ship) document the domain vocabulary and enforce pre-conditions before mutating state.

rule.statement: Each Aggregate Root method that changes state MUST raise a Domain Event (appended to an internal _events list) describing what happened. The application layer collects and dispatches events after the DB transaction commits.
rule.rationale: Events decouple downstream reactions (email, inventory reserve, billing) from the Aggregate. Collecting before dispatch ensures events are only raised when the transaction succeeds.

rule.statement: Aggregates MUST NOT hold direct object references to other Aggregate Roots. Reference by identity (UUID) only. Cross-Aggregate state access goes through Repositories or Domain Services.
rule.rationale: Object references create implicit coupling and allow bypass of the other Aggregate's invariants. Identity references keep transaction boundaries clean and enable eventual consistency across Aggregates.

rule.statement: Aggregate invariants MUST be expressed as failing unit tests before implementation. Each invariant becomes a test: test_cannot_place_empty_order, test_cannot_ship_unpaid_order, test_cannot_modify_placed_order.
rule.rationale: Tests pin the invariants as living documentation and catch regressions. Writing tests first forces explicit invariant identification before coding begins.
